event.event_summary: Synthetic hard-case fixture: a cybersecurity platform sells off after earnings. The replay intentionally places one plausible narrative at rank #1 while later validation supports rank #2.

narratives.title: AI module adoption slowdown
narratives.narrative: The selloff is driven by concern that adoption of Lyra's newer AI security module is slowing after an initially strong launch.
narratives.mechanism: If the AI add-on attaches to fewer renewals, investors would reduce product-cycle revenue expectations and compress the growth multiple.
expected_observables: Management discusses slower AI module attach rates in follow-up commentary
Analysts reduce add-on revenue assumptions within 30 days
Pipeline commentary shifts from expansion demand to product packaging

supporting_evidence.claim: Management said the AI security module remained early in rollout and that attach-rate data was still limited.
supporting_evidence.title: Synthetic transcript excerpt
supporting_evidence.claim_extracted: Management said the AI security module remained early in rollout and that attach-rate data was still limited.

supporting_evidence.claim: The company highlighted AI module pilots but did not quantify contribution to current-quarter revenue.
supporting_evidence.title: Synthetic earnings release
supporting_evidence.claim_extracted: The company highlighted AI module pilots but did not quantify contribution to current-quarter revenue.

narratives.title: Renewal discounting pressure
narratives.narrative: The selloff reflects concern that Lyra is preserving renewal volume by discounting enterprise contracts more aggressively.
narratives.mechanism: Higher renewal discounts would pressure billings quality and future gross retention, even if reported revenue remains resilient.
expected_observables: Analysts cut net retention or billings quality assumptions within 30 days
Follow-up commentary focuses on enterprise renewal concessions
The stock underperforms cybersecurity peers over the next 20 trading days

supporting_evidence.claim: Early market coverage framed the selloff around discounting and renewal quality rather than product adoption.
supporting_evidence.title: Synthetic market note
supporting_evidence.claim_extracted: Early market coverage framed the selloff around discounting and renewal quality rather than product adoption.

supporting_evidence.claim: Executives acknowledged longer renewal negotiations with large enterprise customers.
supporting_evidence.title: Synthetic transcript excerpt
supporting_evidence.claim_extracted: Executives acknowledged longer renewal negotiations with large enterprise customers.

supporting_evidence.claim: Future validation fixture: several analysts later reduced net retention and billings quality assumptions.
supporting_evidence.title: Synthetic future analyst revision
supporting_evidence.claim_extracted: Future validation fixture: several analysts later reduced net retention and billings quality assumptions.

contradicting_evidence.claim: Reported current-quarter revenue exceeded consensus expectations.
contradicting_evidence.title: Synthetic earnings release
contradicting_evidence.claim_extracted: Reported current-quarter revenue exceeded consensus expectations.

narratives.title: Sector budget freeze
narratives.narrative: The move reflects a broader pullback in cybersecurity software spending rather than a Lyra-specific issue.
narratives.mechanism: If security budgets are tightening across the sector, peer-correlated weakness would explain much of the event move.
expected_observables: Cybersecurity peers trade down by similar amounts
Sector commentary emphasizes budget tightening rather than Lyra execution

supporting_evidence.claim: The synthetic cybersecurity peer basket was negative during the event window.
supporting_evidence.title: Synthetic price feed
supporting_evidence.claim_extracted: The synthetic cybersecurity peer basket was negative during the event window.

contradicting_evidence.claim: Lyra underperformed its synthetic cybersecurity peer median by roughly seven percentage points.
contradicting_evidence.title: Synthetic price feed
contradicting_evidence.claim_extracted: Lyra underperformed its synthetic cybersecurity peer median by roughly seven percentage points.

narratives.title: Overreaction to billing transition
narratives.narrative: The market may be overreacting to a temporary billing-model transition that does not impair long-term demand.
narratives.mechanism: If contract-duration and invoicing changes are temporary, the initial selloff could reverse as billings comparability improves.
expected_observables: Management clarifies billing duration mix in follow-up commentary
Revenue estimates stabilize after the first wave of revisions
The stock recovers relative to peers after billing concerns fade

supporting_evidence.claim: Full-year free cash flow guidance was maintained despite a billing-duration transition.
supporting_evidence.title: Synthetic earnings release
supporting_evidence.claim_extracted: Full-year free cash flow guidance was maintained despite a billing-duration transition.
